name: list
title: List
description: Native semantic lists — no ARIA listbox, no Base UI. List renders <ol>/<ul> with token-driven decimal, disc, or unstyled markers (unstyled adds role="list" so Safari/VoiceOver keeps announcing it as a list once list-style is removed). DescriptionList/DescriptionTerm/DescriptionDetails renders <dl>/<dt>/<dd> for term-detail pairs, in stacked or two-column inline layouts. Logical properties throughout, so markers and indentation flip automatically under RTL.
useWhen: A genuine ordered or unordered sequence of items (steps, requirements, documents)
A nav-like or link list that should not show bullets but must still announce as a list (use variant="unstyled")
A set of term/detail pairs — contact details, a case summary, key-value metadata (use DescriptionList)
avoidWhen: A list of interactive choices the user selects from (use RadioGroup, ComboBox, or DropdownMenu instead)
A collection of media-rich cards or articles (use Collection or CardGroup)
Tabular data with rows and columns (use Table)

keyboard: Static content — List, ListItem, DescriptionList, DescriptionTerm, and DescriptionDetails never take focus and add no keyboard behavior
Any interactive element placed inside an item (a Link, a Button) keeps its own native keyboard contract
notes: ordered/unordered keep native list-style markers, so the <ol>/<ul> is never stripped of list semantics.

unstyled removes list-style for a nav-like list but adds an explicit role="list" — Safari/VoiceOver silently drops list semantics from a marker-less <ul>/<ol> otherwise (see packages/core/src/reset.css for the same fix applied to raw HTML).
Markers use marker:text-muted-foreground, a token, and convey structure only (never a status or a color-only distinction), so they carry no meaning that forced-colors mode could remove.
DescriptionTerm/DescriptionDetails render real <dt>/<dd>, preserving the browser's built-in term-to-detail association without any added ARIA.

All indentation uses logical properties (ps-*), so markers and the two-column inline DescriptionList layout mirror automatically under dir="rtl" with no AmbientDirection needed — these are native elements, not a Base UI primitive.
